heading:
Core
Values
description: A merging of Filipino martial philosophy and technical craftsmanship.

quotes.text: From one thing, know ten thousand things.
quotes.attribution: Miyamoto Musashi (The Book of Five Rings)

values.title: Craftsmanship
values.body: What separates good enough and great is invisible to most. It's the attention to details.

values.title: Precision, Simplicity & Directness
values.body: Whether in coding or combat, every action must be deliberate. The simplest and most direct path is often the most effective. Unnecessary complexity is sloppiness, and sloppiness compounds into failure.

values.title: Discipline & Mastery
values.body: Mastery makes difficult things look easy or effortless. But the path to mastery is far from easy. It's showing up daily, doing the work, and refining what came before.

values.title: Continuous Flow & Improvement
values.body: Close-mindedness leads to stagnation. Failing to move could be fatal. If it's not working don't force it. There's always a better way. Find it.

nudge.text: If this resonates with how you think, either about producing quality work or regarding martial arts training, let's talk.
